rulings.Case Title: SHIRLEY ERICKSON et al VS. A.W.CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION Joinder In Defendant Warren Pumps, Llcs Motion To Dismiss
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, NOVEMBER 20, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 1, Defendant Zurn Industries, LLC¿s joinder in Warren Pumps LLC¿s motion to dismiss action is off calendar as moot given the ruling on the underlying motion. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: SHIRLEY ERICKSON et al VS. A.W.CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION And Notice Of Motion To Dismiss Action
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, NOVEMBER 20, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 1, Defendant Warren Pumps LLC¿s motion to dismiss action is granted. No opposition filed. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 10:15am, not 9:30am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: JIM KELLY et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, NOVEMBER 20, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 2, Defendant Rheem¿s Motion for Summary Adjudication of Plaintiffs¿ False Representation cause of action (issue 1) and claim for punitive damages (issue 2) is denied in part and granted in part. Defendant did not sustain its initial burden on the False Representation cause of action. Therefore, the Court denies the Motion for Summary Adjudication as to False Representation. Defendant did sustain its initial burden on the punitive damages issues. Plaintiffs failed to present evidence to create a triable issue whether Defendant is liable for punitive damages. Therefore, the Court grants the Motion for Summary Adjudication as to punitive damages. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 10:15am not 9:30am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: LESLIE MORRIS et al VS. ALTA BUILDING MATERIAL COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, NOVEMBER 20, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 3, Defendant SPX¿s Motion for Summary Judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden as to causation, and Plaintiffs failed to present evidence to create a triable issue whether Decedent¿s asbestos exposure is attributable to Defendant. In light of the Court¿s ruling as to Defendant¿s Motion for Summary Judgment, Defendant SPX¿s Motion for Summary Adjudication as to Plaintiffs¿ causes of action for False Representation and Intentional Tort and claim for punitive damages is moot. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 10:15am not 9:30am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
